hints.prompt: In natural selection, what must be true for some heritable traits to become more common over generations?

choices.label: A
choices.text: An external condition must make some variants survive or reproduce better than others

choices.label: B
choices.text: All individuals reproduce equally regardless of their traits

choices.label: C
choices.text: Traits change during an individual's lifetime to meet its needs

choices.label: D
choices.text: The population must contain no heritable variation

hints.rationale: Selection requires an external condition that favors some heritable variants over others.

hints.prompt: The description specifies an environmental factor 'that influences which traits are favored.' This clue tells you the item is which of the following?

choices.label: A
choices.text: a numerical measure of one genotype's reproductive success

choices.label: B
choices.text: an external cause that biases survival and reproduction

choices.label: C
choices.text: the pool of genetic differences within a population

choices.label: D
choices.text: a burst of new species arising from one ancestor

hints.prompt: The described item is an external environmental factor. Which two options can be eliminated because they instead describe the population's own genetics or reproductive success?

choices.label: B

choices.label: C

choices.label: D
choices.text: relative fitness and adaptive radiation

hints.rationale: Relative fitness (reproductive success) and genetic variation (internal diversity) are properties of the population, not external environmental factors.

hints.prompt: During aerobic respiration of glucose, where is the large majority of ATP generated?

hints.prompt: Osmosis moves water across a membrane in which direction?

hints.rationale: Nondisjunction is a segregation error and recombination frequency is a distance measure; neither is the exchange described.

hints.prompt: When two interacting species repeatedly evolve in response to each other, each new adaptation favors a counter-adaptation in the other. This reciprocal change is a form of:

choices.label: A
choices.text: change driven only by random drift

choices.label: B
choices.text: change guided by human breeders

choices.label: C
choices.text: coevolution between interacting species

choices.label: D

hints.prompt: To qualify as an 'arms race,' the correct answer must include which feature?

choices.label: B

choices.label: C

choices.label: D

hints.prompt: A student might confuse this with 'punctuated equilibrium.' Why is punctuated equilibrium NOT an arms race?

choices.label: B

choices.label: C
choices.text: because it involves only viruses

choices.label: D
choices.text: because it is exactly the same thing as an arms race

hints.rationale: Punctuated equilibrium is a tempo-of-change pattern, not a two-species escalation.
